title: Executive Order 9608—Providing for the Termination of the Office of War Information, and for the Disposition of Its Functions and of Certain Functions of the Office of Inter-American Affairs

flags.justification: The Secretary of State's discretion to transfer or terminate functions of the interim Service is transitional wind-down authority over an explicitly temporary body, not ongoing operational concentration.
A clear chain of responsibility with specific, dated termination milestones (September 15 and December 31, 1945) constitutes a well-structured, staged wind-down.
Multiple distinct transfers (OWI termination, Office of Inter-American Affairs functions, Bureau of Special Services) all coherently serve one purpose: winding down the wartime information apparatus.
An internal government reorganization, not a third-party compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
The order itself is a sunset-implementing mechanism, with explicit, staged termination dates (September 15 and December 31, 1945) for every body it creates.
No existing oversight is removed; this is a careful, staged demobilization.
An internal reorganization with no third-party compliance regime.

The Interim International Information Service is narrowly scoped and explicitly self-terminating by December 31, 1945; the text does not claim broad or ongoing authority beyond that transitional purpose.
Functions are carefully, explicitly reallocated among the State Department, Bureau of the Budget, and Treasury (for final liquidation) with specific criteria for each.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. A well-designed, staged wind-down order for the wartime propaganda/information apparatus.
